calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2023 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2022 Off Calendar

Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
You Liang Song (415.799.6055; songyouliang@yahoo.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Jeff Dinn, Provisional Mandarin Interpreter.
Sharon Nagle, Esq. (925.933.7777; Snagle@bpmnj.com) of Bold, Polisner, Maddow, Nelson & Judson for Moving Party/Defendant (via Zoom videoconference).

The Court noted there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule. The Court allows oral argument.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HONORABLE MICHELLE TONG'S Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's (HON. MICHELLE TONG) demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

A demurrer tests the legal sufficiency of the allegations in the complaint as a matter of law. (Lewis v. Safeway, Inc. (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 385, 391.) Plaintiff brought this civil suit against Defendant for her judicial decision in Plaintiff's case. Defendant now brings this demurrer on the grounds of judicial immunity.

"[T]he doctrine of civil immunity of the judiciary in the performance of judicial functions is deeply rooted in California law." (Howard v. Drapkin (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 843, 852 [internal quotations omitted].) "The decisions of this state uniformly and consistently grant immunity from civil suit to judges in the exercise of their judicial functions. That is true even if the acts are in excess of the jurisdiction of the judge and are alleged to have been done maliciously and corruptly." (Tagliavia v. County of Los Angeles (1980) 112 Cal.App.3d 759, 761.) Judicial immunity is overcome in only two sets of actions: (1) a judge is not immune from liability for nonjudicial actions; and (2) a judge is not immune for actions, though judicial in nature, taken in the complete absence of all jurisdiction. (Mireles v. Waco (1991) 502 U.S. 9, 11-12.)

Here, Plaintiff's claims against Defendant are for her actions taken in her official capacity as a Superior Court Judge. Defendant's actions were taken in the performance of judicial functions and are thus judicial actions. The Defendant exercised her authority to make a ruling on a case over which she presided. The exceptions to judicial immunity do not apply here. (See id.) Accordingly, Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/VJ.CC)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Continued To Nov-18-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
